steps.title: Identify the parallel structure
steps.content: The friend says his soul feels calm from the “lack of anvil and strife” (conflict). The speaker responds directly by describing the kestrel, which “mutes his mating-note to please the harmony of this sweet silence.” This is the speaker showing a natural parallel to what the friend just experienced.

steps.title: Match the parallel to the choices
steps.content: C is correct because the kestrel example functions to present nature as aligning with their experience—the bird silences itself to maintain the same harmony the friend just described. A, B, and D either misread the passage (B falsely claims the friend isn't sharing; A introduces the irrelevant idea of mystery; D suggests peace means different things to different people, which isn't discussed). That matches choice C — C is correct.
